'''Ellor''' was a [[Duros/Legends|Duros]] [[Smuggler/Legends|smuggler]] who worked for [[Billey]].

==History==
Ellor met [[Talon Karrde]] shortly after graduating from a university on [[Duros/Legends|Duros]]. He joined with Karrde on a smuggling job that nearly ended in disaster when they were confronted by an [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Immobilizer 418 Interdictor-class Heavy Cruiser|Interdictor]] shortly after picking up a load of [[Gy'lan/Legends|Gy'lan]] [[Spice/Legends|spice]]. Karrde managed to get them to safety, but Ellor became enamored with the smuggling life.<ref name="TTS" /> He joined Talon Karrde's [[Smugglers' Alliance]] to fight against [[Grand Admiral/Legends|Grand Admiral]] [[Mitth'raw'nuruodo/Legends|Thrawn]] after Imperial troops raided a smuggler meet on [[Trogan/Legends|Trogan]]. Ellor had ties to some Duros repair yards which were employed by the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]], and on one occasion "borrowed" a pair of Republic [[DP20 gunship/Legends|DP-20 gunships]] for use in a [[Raid on Bilbringi|raid]] against Thrawn's shipyard at [[Bilbringi system/Legends|Bilbringi]] that he helped [[Mazzic]] orchestrate.<ref name="The Last Command">''[[The Last Command]]''</ref>

Ellor's group later participated in the [[Battle of Bilbringi (9 ABY)|Battle of Bilbringi]] along with other members of the Smugglers' Alliance in an effort to procure a [[crystal gravfield trap]] to sell to the New Republic. Though their plans were disrupted by the arrival of New Republic and Imperial fleets, the smugglers were able to help the New Republic by launching a diversionary assault on a [[Golan II Space Defense SpaceGun|Golan II]] battle station.<ref name="The Last Command" />

==Behind the scenes==
Ellor was incorrectly portrayed as a [[Sullustan/Legends|Sullustan]] in the comic adaption of the [[Star Wars: The Thrawn Trilogy|Thrawn Trilogy]], and was named ''Par'tah'' in the second issue of ''[[The Last Command]]''.
